prose:
**Oxygen HOMO–LUMO gap (frontier-orbital energy spacing)** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (density-fit RKS/UKS in PySCF), **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 3 (triplet ³Σ_g⁻ ground state of O₂). Geometry: fixed-point single-point calculation on the provided molecular-oxygen structure from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/oxygen/oxygen.xyz`. SCF converged in 7 cycles; integrity check passed (trustworthy = true). HOMO (index 15) = −8.8739616 eV; LUMO (index 16) = −3.5859031 eV. Gap = 5.28805856634771 eV (Koopmans orbital energy difference, not an excitation energy). Derived properties: vertical ionization potential 8.874 eV, vertical electron affinity 3.586 eV, electronegativity 6.230 eV, chemical hardness 2.644 eV. The frontier degeneracy (HOMO = HOMO-1 at −8.874 eV) reflects the two unpaired π* electrons of the open-shell triplet.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
